query: Layoffs.fyi tracker big tech May 2026 latest entries
answer:
## Layoffs.fyi Tracker: Big Tech Layoffs — May 2026 Latest Entries

### Layoffs.fyi Cumulative Statistics (as of ~May 9, 2026)

| Year | Tech Employees Laid Off | Companies with Layoffs |
|------|------------------------|----------------------|
| **2026** | **101,550** | **120** |
| 2025 | 124,281 | 272 |
| 2024 | 152,922 | 551 |
| 2023 | 264,320 | 1,193 |
| 2022 | 165,269 | 1,064 |

As of approximately May 8–9, 2026, over **101,550 tech employees** have been laid off across **120 companies** in 2026. Broader counts (including adjacent industries) reach **128,000+** per Yahoo Tech.

---

### May 2026 Specific Entries

**Cloudflare** — May 8, 2026
- **1,100 employees** cut (~20% of workforce)
- CEO Matthew Prince cited AI making jobs "obsolete"; internal AI usage up 600% in three months
- Record Q1 revenue of $639.8M (up 34% YoY) despite cuts
- Sources: TechCrunch, San Francisco Chronicle

**Coinbase** — May 5, 2026
- **~700 employees** cut (14% of staff)
- CEO Brian Armstrong cited crypto market volatility and AI-driven efficiency
- Severance costs: $50–60 million; flattening org structure
- Source: TechCrunch

**Meta** — Announced April 23; first wave targets **May 20, 2026**
- **8,000 employees** to be cut (10% of workforce)
- 6,000 open roles frozen/unfilled
- Earlier 2026 rounds: ~1,000 (Reality Labs, January), several hundred (March)
- **Total Meta 2026 layoffs: ~10,000+**
- Sources: TechCrunch, Reuters, Bloomberg, CNBC

**Truecaller** — May 8, 2026
- **70 employees** (~15% of workforce)
- Ad revenue down 44% YoY; net sales down 27%
- Source: TechCrunch

**Disney** — Early May 2026
- Layoffs reported, company touting "Culture of Efficiency"
- Source: Deadline (May 6)

---

### Big Tech 2026 Year-to-Date Summary (through May 9)

| Company | 2026 Total Layoffs | Key Details |
|---------|-------------------|-------------|
| **Amazon** | ~30,000–32,000 | 16,000 (Jan 28) + 16,000 (Apr 21); plans to hire 11,000 engineers |
| **Meta** | ~10,000+ | Multiple rounds; largest wave (8,000) starts May 20 |
| **Microsoft** | Up to ~8,750 | Voluntary buyouts/retirement ("Rule of 70") offered Apr 23 |
| **Block (Square)** | 4,000+ | ~50% of workforce (Feb 26); AI cited |
| **Atlassian** | 1,600 | Mar 11–12; AI and enterprise pivot |
| **Cloudflare** | 1,100 | May 8; AI-driven restructuring |
| **Snap** | 1,000 | Apr 15 (16% of workforce); AI cited |
| **Epic Games** | 1,000 | Mar 24; Fortnite engagement decline |
| **eBay** | 800 | Feb 26 |
| **Coinbase** | ~700 | May 5; crypto volatility + AI |

---

### Macro Context

- **AI is the dominant driver**: 26% of April 2026 job cuts were explicitly attributed to AI (CBS News, May 8). Companies report record revenues while cutting staff, citing AI productivity gains.
- **Tech is an outlier**: Overall corporate layoffs are down 10% YoY, but tech layoffs are accelerating (WSJ, May 8).
- **Spending shift**: Headlines note "tens of thousands" of tech workers replaced by $725 billion in AI infrastructure spending directed at just four companies (24/7 Wall St, May 7).
- **Scale concern**: CNBC (May 1) flagged 20,000+ job cuts at Meta and Microsoft alone as evidence the "AI-driven labor crisis is here."

key_facts: Layoffs.fyi tracks 101,550 tech employees laid off across 120 companies in 2026 as of ~May 9, 2026.
Cloudflare cut 1,100 employees (~20% of workforce) on May 8, 2026, citing AI making jobs obsolete; internal AI usage up 600% in three months.
Coinbase cut ~700 employees (14% of staff) on May 5, 2026, citing crypto volatility and AI efficiency.
Meta announced 8,000 layoffs (10% of workforce) on April 23, with first wave targeting May 20, 2026; total 2026 Meta layoffs ~10,000+.
Truecaller cut 70 employees (~15% of workforce) on May 8, 2026, due to 44% YoY decline in ad revenue.
Amazon has cut ~30,000-32,000 jobs in 2026 (16,000 in January + 16,000 in April), making it the largest big tech layoff of the year.
Microsoft offered voluntary buyouts to ~8,750 U.S. employees using 'Rule of 70' (age + years of service >= 70) around April 23, 2026.
26% of April 2026 job cuts were explicitly attributed to AI according to CBS News (May 8).
Overall corporate layoffs are down 10% YoY, but tech sector layoffs are accelerating (WSJ, May 8).
Broader counts including adjacent industries place 2026 layoffs at 128,000+ (Yahoo Tech).
Block (Square/Cash App) cut 4,000+ employees (~50% of workforce) in February 2026, one of the most dramatic single cuts.
Companies are simultaneously reporting record revenues while cutting staff, citing AI productivity gains as justification.
